sections.heading: 1. Short Recap
sections.body: The Mets (3-3) are looking to rebound from two consecutive losses against the Cardinals, where they totaled only three runs. The Giants (2-4) also face challenges, coming off a blowout loss to the Padres after securing a win in their previous game.

sections.heading: 2. Pitching Matchup
sections.body: David Peterson (LHP) takes the mound for the Mets. He has a 0.00 ERA and a 1.50 WHIP over 5.1 innings this season, generating a 12% K rate and an 8% BB rate. In contrast, Robbie Ray (LHP) pitches for the Giants with a 3.38 ERA and 0.94 WHIP over 5.1 innings, striking out 19% of batters with no walks. Peterson's underlying metrics suggest potential for better performance with a lower expected ERA.

sections.heading: 3. Lineup Comparison
sections.body: The Mets' projected lineup is outperforming the Giants in several metrics: they feature a 103 wRC+ compared to San Francisco's 73, a barrel percentage of 7.8% versus 3.3%, and maintain a better hard-hit rate (40.1% to 34.9%). Notable hitters like Juan Soto (.346 AVG, .952 OPS) and Francisco Alvarez (.231 AVG, .913 OPS) lead the way, while the Giants struggle with contributions from several players, notably Helio Ramos and Luis Arraez.

sections.heading: 4. Bullpen
sections.body: The Mets possess a stronger bullpen stats-wise, with a season ERA of 2.87 and a last 14-day ERA of 2.50, yielding a 25.5% strikeout rate. Conversely, the Giants' bullpen sports a 3.40 ERA and a last 14-day ERA of 3.74. This edge in the bullpen could be crucial, especially in a tight game late.

sections.heading: 5. Key Edges
sections.body: Mets have clear advantages in offensive metrics, including a significantly higher walk rate (12.4% vs. 6.7%) and a lower strikeout rate (21.4% vs. 23.0%). Additionally, Peterson's ability to limit hard contact (40% hard-hit rate against) contrasts with Ray's vulnerability (35.3% hard-hit rate). These factors suggest the Mets can capitalize on scoring opportunities.

sections.heading: 6. Today's Pick
sections.body: With superior metrics across pitching, batting, and bullpen performance, the New York Mets are positioned well to secure a victory against the San Francisco Giants.

writeup.pickSummary: The Mets enter today's game with a favorable overall matchup, highlighted by David Peterson's strong performance and a potent offense that has consistently outperformed the Giants in critical statistical categories. The Mets' robust bullpen and offensive edge, particularly in plate discipline, increases their chances of winning. Therefore, the recommended play is on the Mets' moneyline, leveraging the identified advantages.
writeup.officialPick: Today's Pick: New York Mets Moneyline

lastMeeting.summary: On 2026-03-31, the Mets lost to the St. Louis Cardinals 0-3 at Busch Stadium.
lastMeeting.recapHeadline: With Cards pitcher in a bind, Gold Glover's hose puts out the fire
recapNotes: For the second night in a row at Busch Stadium, a Cardinals starter pitched into the sixth inning but saw his outing screech to a halt before he could record an out in the frame.
Andre Pallante cruised through five innings before the heart of the Mets’ order started to get to him in the sixth.

source.label: Last meeting recap
source.headline: With Cards pitcher in a bind, Gold Glover's hose puts out the fire

sections.heading: 1. Short Recap
sections.body: Mets 3-2, St. Louis Cardinals 3-2, Busch Stadium. Last meeting: On 2026-03-31, the Mets lost to the St. Louis Cardinals 0-3 at Busch Stadium. Recap source: With Cards pitcher in a bind, Gold Glover's hose puts out the fire.

sections.heading: 2. Pitching Matchup
sections.body: Freddy Peralta (7.20 ERA, 1.20 WHIP, 2026 - 5.0 IP) vs Matthew Liberatore (1.80 ERA, 1.80 WHIP, 2026 - 5.0 IP).

sections.heading: 3. Lineup Comparison
sections.body: Lineups are confirmed. Team offense: NYM wRC+ 103, xwOBA 0.332, K% 21.4%. St. Louis Cardinals wRC+ 106, xwOBA 0.347, K% 19.5%.

sections.heading: 4. Bullpen
sections.body: Bullpen check: Mets ERA 2.87, xFIP 3.72, WHIP 1.26. St. Louis Cardinals ERA 5.09, xFIP 5.22, WHIP 1.65.

sections.heading: 5. Key Edges
sections.body: Main numbers: NYM wRC+ 103 vs 106, NYM xwOBA 0.332 vs 0.347, NYM bullpen rating 85 vs 69.

sections.heading: 6. Today's Pick
sections.body: Today's Pick: New York Mets Moneyline. Reason: better lineup quality and the stronger bullpen numbers.

writeup.pickSummary: Play the Mets moneyline. The case is simple: better team offense, cleaner bullpen profile, and a favorable recent series result.
